mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 9.0 percent signals severe labor market deterioration and collapsing consumer spending, which forces corporate earnings revisions downward and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 9.0% over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in real sales of existing homes and housing starts confirm broad-based demand recovery in the housing sector, which drives construction and durable goods earnings, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both rise > 2% MoM
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in >60% of cases over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A manufacturing index above 100.0 indicates expansionary industrial output that exceeds baseline capacity, generating immediate positive momentum in cyclical equity sectors and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) when inflation exceeds 5.0% annually.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index above 215.0 reflects entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to tighten policy expectations, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >70% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 215.0.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls directly increase aggregate household income and consumer spending capacity, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 3-month rolling correlation between PAYEMS growth and SPX returns falls below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP contraction below 1.0% and monthly declines in real existing home sales signal a synchronized demand collapse across consumption and investment channels, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth < 1.0% QoQ AND RSAFS declines MoM
failure_condition: Signal triggers but SPX delivers positive 20-day forward returns in >65% of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: A manufacturing index below 98.0 during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply chain constraints and falling output that outweighs rate hike benefits, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Negative signal coincides with positive forward returns in >60% of tightening periods.
